brands.name: Visiting Angels
brands.notes: One of the largest non-medical senior home-care franchises in the US, founded 1998 and franchising since 1998 (Living Assistance Services, Inc.). ~539 US franchised units at end of 2024 with strong long-tenure unit revenues, but the sector carries recurring caregiver wage/overtime litigation risk.

riskFlags.title: Caregiver overtime/minimum-wage collective action against franchisee
riskFlags.detail: Njoroge et al. v. Primacare Partners LLC d/b/a Visiting Angels Baltimore East (D. Md., No. 22-cv-425-BAH) alleges live-in and dayshift caregivers worked over 40 hrs/week without FLSA overtime and that 24-hour caregivers' flat day rates fell below minimum wage; court allowed it to proceed as a collective action. Reflects sector-wide wage-and-hour exposure at the franchisee level.

riskFlags.title: Federal civil monetary penalty at a franchise location
riskFlags.detail: Visiting Angels of Rhode Island agreed to pay $158,000 to settle HHS-OIG allegations of employing an individual excluded from federal health programs (Civil Monetary Penalties Law). Location-level compliance issue, not franchisor-wide.

riskFlags.title: Flat unit growth
riskFlags.detail: Net change of only +1 franchised unit in 2024 (539 outlets), indicating a mature, slow-growth US system rather than rapid expansion.

nationwide.source: Visiting Angels Franchise Development (Living Assistance Services, Inc.)
nationwide.note: Franchise development site states 'Nationwide Territories & Opportunities Are Now Available' with 600+ US franchises; specific market availability checked via territory map tool.

franchiseFee.note: New-franchise fee starts at $51,950; $15,950 is the transfer-only minimum. Prior batch value 15950 superseded by verification audit 2026-06-11.

royaltyPercent.note: Tiered: 3.5% of gross revenues, stepping down to 3.25% above $125K/mo and 3.0% above $225K/mo; monthly minimums apply.

adFundPercent.note: 2.5% of gross revenues or tiered $425-$1,195 monthly minimum.

avgUnitRevenue.note: Item 19 (2024) is presented as a revenue-vs-longevity distribution, not a single AUV; for franchisees operating 73+ months, 20.6% earned $1M-$1.5M annually. $1M is an approximate lower-band figure for mature units, not a system average.

totalUnits.note: 539 franchised outlets, 0 company-owned, at end of 2024 (2025 FDD).

yearFounded.note: Founded 1998 by Larry Meigs and Jeffrey Johnson; Living Assistance Services, Havertown PA.

yearFranchising.note: Began franchising in 1998 in the Philadelphia area.

isGrowing.note: Net +1 US franchised unit in 2024 — essentially flat.

item19Available.note: Item 19 present as a revenue-by-tenure distribution.

exclusiveTerritories.note: Grants a Protected Territory sized by population (up to 325K+).

brands.name: Right at Home
brands.notes: Large in-home senior care franchise founded 1995 (Omaha, NE) and franchising since 2000; ~539 US franchised offices at end of 2024 with disclosed Item 19 net-billings figures. Grew +31 units in 2024 and carries the standard home-care wage-and-hour litigation exposure.

riskFlags.title: Sector-wide caregiver wage-and-hour exposure
riskFlags.detail: In-home care franchises broadly face FLSA/state overtime and minimum-wage claims at the franchisee level (live-in, 24-hour and travel-time pay disputes are common across the senior-care category). No franchisor-wide Right at Home class action was located, but franchisees inherit this category risk.

riskFlags.title: State home-care licensure required
riskFlags.detail: Senior home-care operation requires state-specific home care agency licenses and caregiver background-check/training compliance, which vary by state and add regulatory cost/time to launch.

nationwide.source: Right at Home franchise overview (Franchising.com) / WebSearch sector summary
nationwide.note: Franchises sold across the US (one of the largest home-care franchises in the world, 750+ locations across 5 countries); grants protected territories of ~200,000-350,000 population.

royaltyPercent.note: Greater of 5% of Net Billings or a minimum quarterly royalty.

adFundPercent.note: Brand marketing: greater of 2% on first $1M or 1% on next $2M of Net Billings, plus 1-2% local marketing.

avgUnitRevenue.note: Item 19 (2024): average Net Billings across all offices open 1+ year. Offices open 61+ months averaged $1,848,745 (median $1,386,635).

totalUnits.note: 539 franchised + 12 company-owned at end of 2024 (2025 FDD).

yearFounded.note: Founded 1995 in Fremont, NE by Allen Hager.

veteranDiscount.note: 25% veteran discount on franchise fee reported.

brands.name: Comfort Keepers
brands.notes: In-home senior care franchise (CK Franchising, Inc., Irvine CA) founded 1998 and franchising since 1999; ~578 US franchised + 41 company-owned units (619 total). Multiple franchisee-level wage and 'no-hire' enforcement actions in the network elevate compliance/litigation risk.

riskFlags.title: DOL contempt action over repeated overtime violations at a Comfort Keepers operator
riskFlags.detail: The U.S. Department of Labor sought a contempt order against Tim Paul / TPS Caregiving (operating as Comfort Keepers Home Care) for allegedly continuing to withhold overtime pay from 700+ employees after a 2022 consent judgment in S.D. Indiana barred future violations.

riskFlags.title: California AG $500K judgment over unlawful 'no-hire' clauses
riskFlags.detail: California Attorney General obtained a stipulated judgment requiring Comfort Keepers to pay $500,000 in civil penalties for using unlawful no-hire/non-solicitation and onerous liquidated-damages provisions in client care agreements; a related no-hire-clause investigation continued in 2025.
riskFlags.source: California DOJ / Office of the Attorney General

riskFlags.title: Caregiver overtime class action (Pennsylvania)
riskFlags.detail: PA Minimum Wage Act class action alleged Comfort Keepers paid caregivers a flat hourly rate for all hours and failed to pay 1.5x for hours over 40/week.
riskFlags.source: The Lore Law Firm case summary

nationwide.source: Comfort Keepers Franchising (territories page) / sector summary
nationwide.note: 625+ US franchise locations; franchisor markets available territories nationwide and grants protected territories (~350,000 population).

totalInvestmentMin.note: 2025 FDD Item 7; reported range $116,950-$188,250 (other sources round to $117K-$188K).

franchiseFee.note: $55,000 for first territory; discounts for additional territories and veterans.

adFundPercent.note: 2% national brand fund reported (plus local marketing minimums); one source lists a 4% brand fund — figure may vary, lower/recurring value used.

veteranDiscount.note: Veteran discount on franchise fee offered.

brands.name: Senior Helpers
brands.notes: In-home senior care franchise founded 2002 and franchising since 2005; ~355 US franchised + 5 company-owned units (360 total), growing ~+22 units/year. Discloses detailed Item 19 revenue-by-tenure data; low entry capital requirements relative to the category.

riskFlags.title: Sector-wide caregiver wage-and-hour exposure
riskFlags.detail: Home-care franchisees broadly face FLSA/state overtime, minimum-wage, live-in and travel-time pay claims; no franchisor-wide Senior Helpers class action was located in the searches, but franchisees carry this category-level employment-law risk.

riskFlags.title: State home-care licensure and caregiver compliance required
riskFlags.detail: Operating requires state home care agency licensure, background checks and caregiver training that vary by state, adding regulatory cost and time to launch.

nationwide.note: ~370 US territories; franchisor describes selling franchises 'throughout the country' and grants protected territories of 200,000-350,000 population.

liquidCapitalMin.note: Minimum $50,000 liquid assets ($200,000 net worth) — among lowest in the senior-care category.

royaltyPercent.note: 5% of gross sales year 1; thereafter greater of 5% or minimum periodic payment.

adFundPercent.note: Marketing fund up to 2% of gross sales; currently 1% on first $2M, 0.5% above; plus a brand management fee per period.

avgUnitRevenue.note: Item 19 (2024): average gross revenue for the 232 units open 60+ months (median $1,312,197). Excludes operating costs.

totalUnits.note: 360 total (355 franchised, 5 company-owned) at end of 2024.

yearFounded.note: Founded 2002 by Tony Bonacuse and Peter Ross.

brands.name: BrightStar Care
brands.notes: Higher-acuity home care + medical staffing franchise founded 2002 (Gurnee, IL, Shelly Sun) and franchising since 2005; ~373 franchised + 35 company-owned units at end of 2024, growing +26 units. Highest disclosed Item 19 average revenue in this batch (~$2.46M for first agencies open 12+ months) but carries notable franchisee-relations and wage litigation.

riskFlags.title: Franchisor-vs-franchisee non-compete litigation
riskFlags.detail: BrightStar Franchising, LLC v. Foreside Management Co. (N.D. Ill., No. 1:25-cv-08741, decided Oct 29, 2025): former California franchisees let agreements expire and kept operating; court granted BrightStar a partial preliminary injunction enforcing an 18-month non-compete/non-solicit under Illinois law, rejecting the franchisees' California §16600 defense. Indicates aggressive post-term restrictive-covenant enforcement against exiting franchisees.
riskFlags.source: Goldstein Law Group analysis / Justia docket

riskFlags.title: Caregiver overtime / wage-and-hour claims at franchise operators
riskFlags.detail: Multiple wage suits at BrightStar operators, including a Kentucky caregiver alleging 90+ hour weeks without time-and-a-half overtime, and a California class action alleging unpaid on-call time, denied meal/rest breaks, and unreimbursed expenses.

riskFlags.title: Federal civil monetary penalty at a franchise location
riskFlags.detail: JRC Ventures d/b/a BrightStar Care of Chattanooga paid a $20,374 HHS-OIG settlement (Oct 2024) for employing an individual excluded from federal health programs. Location-level compliance issue.

nationwide.note: Territories available in nearly all 50 states (currently none in HI, MN, NE, NH, OK); typical territory 200,000-300,000 population with 15,000+ aged 65+.

franchiseFee.note: Lower bound: $25,000 for markets under 200K population; $50,000 for 200K-300K population territories.

royaltyPercent.note: 5.25% of monthly Net Billings on non-National Accounts; 6.25% on National Accounts.

adFundPercent.note: Greater of $500/month or 2.5% of prior month's Net Billings.

avgUnitRevenue.note: Item 19 (2024): average revenue for first franchised agencies open 12+ months, across all service lines (personal care, skilled, companion, staffing). Top quartile $4.72M, bottom quartile $967K; avg gross margin 42.4%.

totalUnits.note: 373 franchised (+26 vs 2023) + 35 company-owned at end of 2024.

yearFounded.note: Founded 2002 in Gurnee, IL by Shelly Sun and J.D. Sun.

veteranDiscount.note: $5,000 franchise-fee discount for qualifying military veterans.
